title: Gen Z’s Complex Relationship with AI Revealed in New Gallup Report
summary: A new Gallup report shows Gen Z is growing disillusioned with AI, yet continues to use it extensively. The findings highlight a nuanced relationship as AI becomes more integrated into daily life.
body:
A recent Gallup report based on responses from nearly 1,600 Gen Z individuals aged 14 to 29 across the US reveals a growing disillusionment with AI. Despite this, the digital-native generation continues to use AI tools extensively in school and work environments. The report suggests that while the initial hype around AI is waning, its practical applications remain deeply embedded in daily life.

The findings highlight a complex relationship between Gen Z and AI, marked by both skepticism and reliance. As AI tools become more ubiquitous, younger generations are increasingly aware of their limitations and ethical concerns. This contrasts with earlier optimism, indicating a shift towards a more critical and pragmatic view of AI's role in society.

Moving forward, the report's insights suggest that AI developers and educators must address these concerns to maintain trust and engagement. The ongoing integration of AI in education and workplaces will likely shape how Gen Z interacts with and perceives these technologies in the future. Open questions remain about how this generation will balance their reliance on AI with their growing skepticism.
twitterThread:
Gen Z is over the AI hype 📉

But they’re still using it. A lot.

Here’s the twist:

Gallup surveyed 1,600 Gen Zers (14-29). They’re disillusioned but can’t quit AI.

Why? It’s everywhere. School. Work. Daily life.

Here’s why this matters:

This isn’t a rejection. It’s a wake-up call. Gen Z sees the flaws. But they’re stuck using it anyway.

The wild part?

They’re the most tech-savvy generation. If they’re skeptical, AI has a problem.

Hot take:

AI developers need to listen. Fast. Or Gen Z will lead the backlash.
